channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Google Messages finally reverses an annoying change, making it easier to organize your contacts
item.description: Google Messages users are regaining the ability to add contact names and photos for their contracts locally, following the controversial decision to implement individually-set social media-style profiles in 2024.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: TikTok's Uncertain Future: What Happens if the App is Banned?
item.description: TikTok's future in the US hangs in the balance, and users are scrambling to save their content before it's potentially too late.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Europe takes a second look at Apple’s DMA compliance
item.description: The European Commission (EC) might have at last seen the sense of pulling back on some of its scrutiny of big tech, which could yet benefit Apple and Google. For many, this will be a welcome step, though critics will continue to criticize.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Feldman: Musk and his critics are both wrong about free speech on X
item.description: While Musk has the right to demonetize MAGA critics for their opinions, he isn't truly applying the First Amendment
item.dc:creator: Noah Feldman, The Mercury News

item.title: HPE Aruba targets retailers with IoT, edge computing launches
item.description: HPE Aruba Networking has rolled out a variety of new products for retailers that are aimed at boosting connectivity for in-store and IoT networks, improving analytics, and streamlining management.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI experts tamp down hype, expect challenges for 2025
item.description: A trio of AI researchers and experts in Lithuania and England have tossed some cold water on the hype surrounding generative AI and predict that in 2025, companies and investors will have to lower their expectations regarding what the technology and large language models (LLM) can do.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to stop doomscrolling and get your motivation back
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Delia Cai, Fast Company

item.title: Biden's administration proposes new rules on exporting AI chips, provoking an industry pushback
item.description: The Biden administration is proposing a new framework for the exporting of the advanced computer chips used to develop artificial intelligence, an attempt to balance national security concerns about the technology with the economic interests of producers and other countries.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The next AI wave
item.description: The next wave of artificial intelligence (AI) adoption is already under way, as AI agents — AI applications that can function independently and execute complex workflows with minimal or limited direct human oversight — are being rolled out across the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok app a ‘national security risk,' Sen. Mark Kelly says
item.description: “I imagine [there's gonna] be a disruption in the service here, you know, starting on the 19th,” Kelly told CBS News’s Margaret Brennan on “Face the Nation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: A young gorilla rescued from aircraft hold recovers at Istanbul zoo
item.description: A young gorilla rescued from a plane’s cargo hold is recovering at an Istanbul zoo while wildlife officers consider returning him to his natural habitat.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hydrogen-powered rescue truck just smashed a world record, and it only spits out water
item.description: A truck powered by hydrogen traveled 1,806 miles on a single tank of hydrogen with zero emissions and set a new Guinness World Record.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 4 ways Apple should upgrade its privacy and security game in 2025
item.description: Every year, Apple adds additional privacy features that make its products the most secure in the world. Most recently, in iOS 18, Apple added the ability to limit Contacts access to only the select connections you choose.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Zuckerberg says Biden officials would 'scream,' 'curse' at Meta team over COVID takedown requests
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg said Biden administration officials would “scream” and “curse” at his employees when they disagreed with the government’s takedown requests over pandemic-related content.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The top five procurement themes for 2025
item.description: 2025 will see continued focus on supply chain resiliency, often revolving around support for and investment in the domestic industrial base. The post The top five procurement themes for 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: New malware justifies Apple’s locked-down security strategy
item.description: Apple has told us Macs aren’t secure enough and it continues working to improve their security, as it does across all of its platforms. But a newly identified malware attack confirms that third-party developers can sometimes be a weak link in the perimeter.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cohere goes ‘North’ with agentic AI
item.description: Large language model (LLM) provider Cohere has unveiled its new agentic AI offering — North — a low-code platform that will allow enterprises to build and deploy agents across different business functions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: More than 4% PC shipment growth predicted for 2025, but not for what you expect, says IDC
item.description: PC sales certainly weren’t going gangbusters in 2024: They only grew a paltry 1% over 2023. According to new figures from IDC, vendors shipped 262.7 million PCs in 2024. But things did pick up a bit in Q4 2024: Shipments grew 1.8% over the prior year, reaching 68.9 million.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What to know as TikTok argues in front of Supreme Court
item.description: TikTok is hoping to invalidate a law that would force it to divest from its Chinese parent company or face a ban.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers to seek GAO review of TSA’s biometrics, facial recognition use
item.description: TSA has expanded its use of facial recognition technology to more than 80 airports across the country in recent years. The post Lawmakers to seek GAO review of TSA’s biometrics, facial recognition use first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: How to use the new Lock object in C# 13
item.description: The C# programming language has provided support for thread synchronization using the lock keyword since its earliest versions. By using a lock statement, you ensure that only one thread can execute the body of the statement at a time. Any other thread is blocked until the lock is released.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The ‘Worst in Show’ CES products put your data at risk and cause waste, privacy advocates say
item.description: So much of the technology showcased at CES includes gadgets made to improve consumers’ lives — whether by leveraging AI to make devices that help people become more efficient, by creating companions to cure loneliness or by providing tools that help people with mental and physical health.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: GlassFish update fixes restart hangs, random 403 responses
item.description: The Eclipse Foundation has released GlassFish 7.0.21, an update of the enterprise Java application server that focuses on fixing a few tenacious bugs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Jeff Bezos and Lauren Sanchez to marry in Italy: Source
item.description: Amazon billionaire and his blushing bride-to-be, Lauren Sanchez, held a lovely dinner party over the holidays at Matsuhisa in Aspen.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Federal cyber leaders on building national resilience
item.description: How are you building resilience into your agency’s cybersecurity? The post Federal cyber leaders on building national resilience first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Closing the digital gap: The importance of secure development practices in government IT modernization
item.description: In today's fast-paced digital landscape, effective government IT modernization is crucial for agencies to achieve their missions. The post Closing the digital gap: The importance of secure development practices in government IT modernization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Nvidia launches blueprints to help jump start AI projects
item.description: Give Nvidia credit, it’s not just talking up big ideas, it’s helping its customers achieve them. The vendor recently issued designs for AI factories after hyping up the idea for several months.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: FBI's new warning about AI-driven scams that are after your cash
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Preventing this insidious email forwarding scam that will drain your bank account
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says business email compromise scams are a serious threat, as shown by Teresa W.'s near loss of thousands of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oracle offers price-performance boost with Exadata X11M update
item.description: The new version of Oracle’s Exadata platform, X11M, is now generally available, and promises improved performance on generative AI tasks such as retrieval augmented generation (RAG) for the same price as the old version.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why the C programming language still rules
item.description: The C programming language has been alive and kicking since 1972, and it still reigns as one of the essential building blocks of our software-studded world.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI Outperforms Human Experts in Ovarian Cancer Detection
item.description: Researchers at Karolinska Institute show that artificial intelligence can detect ovarian cancer with 86% accuracy, outperforming human experts and less experienced personnel.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation
